description: Night Mode for the Paper & Gold portfolio — dark-first 'Night Paper' theme (warm blacks, cream text, G3 gold #C9A227), navbar sun/moon toggle, persisted preference, full token migration. PRD: tasks/prd-night-mode.md

userStories.title: Two-theme token foundation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all theme colors defined once as CSS custom properties so both themes derive from one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: :root defines light tokens: --bg #FAF7F1, --bg-alt #F3EEE4, --bg-card #FFFDF9, --bg-img #EFE9DD, --text #221E16, --text-muted #6E6659, --accent #9C7A2A, --accent-text #8A6D1B, --hairline rgba(34,30,22,0.10)

.dark overrides: --bg #161310, --bg-alt #1B1712, --bg-card #242018, --bg-img #2A251C, --text #F0EAE0, --text-muted rgba(240,234,224,0.65), --accent #C9A227, --accent-text #D9B84A, --hairline rgba(240,234,224,0.12)
The 12 stale .dark rules in src/index.css are removed or rewritten in terms of tokens
Light mode renders visually identical to before (screenshot hero + one section, compare)
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Navbar sun/moon toggle, dark-first persistence
userStories.description: As a visitor, I land on the black Night Paper look by default and can switch to light with a familiar sun/moon button; my choice is remembered.
acceptanceCriteria: Round icon button in desktop navbar (right side, near Resume) and in the mobile menu: moon icon shown in light mode, sun icon in night mode
Clicking toggles the 'dark' class on document.documentElement and saves 'light'/'dark' to localStorage key 'theme'
Inline <script> in index.html <head> applies persisted theme before first paint (no flash); missing/unknown value defaults to DARK
Button has aria-label ('Switch to light mode' / 'Switch to night mode') and visible focus ring in both themes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP (dev server http://localhost:5176): toggle works, reload keeps choice, cleared localStorage lands on dark
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Global stylesheet (index.css) on tokens
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the site shell — navbar, hero, buttons, section headings, timeline, skills groups, contact form, scroll-top — correct in both themes.
acceptanceCriteria: All hardcoded colors in src/index.css replaced with var(--…) tokens (intentionally fixed colors like the green status dot may stay)
Hero photo edge gradient uses var(--bg) so the portrait blends into the background in both themes
Navbar background/blur, logo, links, and mobile menu themed correctly in both modes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP in BOTH themes: hero, navbar, buttons, one timeline entry
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Light components on tokens
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want About, Experience, Skills, Coursework, Contact, and Projects to render correctly in night mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Inline hex/rgba styles in About.jsx, Experience.jsx, Skills.jsx, Coursework.jsx, Contact.jsx, Projects.jsx replaced with var(--…) tokens
Project cards: card surface, tag chips, GitHub and Open Live Website buttons legible in both themes
Coursework 'Current' pill and completed-credits banner legible in both themes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP in BOTH themes: each of the six sections
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Featured (ToWin) section on tokens
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the flagship ToWin showcase to look deliberate in night mode.
acceptanceCriteria: FeaturedProject.jsx <style> block converted to tokens: section bg, tag, title, feature cards, tech chips, buttons, architecture card, preview border/shadow
ToWin screenshot gets a subtle hairline border in night mode so the light image doesn't glare
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP in BOTH themes
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Dark bands and seams in night mode
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want Beyond Code and the Footer (permanently dark) to feel continuous with night mode instead of separate slabs.
acceptanceCriteria: In night mode, seams between the dark bands and adjacent night sections use the hairline token — visible but subtle
Beyond Code cards and Footer keep G3 gold (#C9A227) accents in both themes
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP in BOTH themes: scroll the seams above/below both bands
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)

userStories.title: Accessibility and full-sweep verification
userStories.description: As any visitor, I can read and navigate everything in both themes on desktop and mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Small gold text meets >=4.5:1 contrast in both themes (#8A6D1B on #FAF7F1; #D9B84A on #161310 — compute and record the ratios in notes)
Focus rings visible in both themes
prefers-reduced-motion behavior unchanged (scroll-behavior auto, mobile menu animation off)
Verify in browser using Playwright MCP: full top-to-bottom scroll in BOTH themes at 1280px and 390px widths; no unreadable text, invisible borders, or light-mode remnants in night mode
Lint passes (npx eslint src)
Build passes (npm run build)
